pr.title: Remove all the incbins in sdata and resolve pointers to labels
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Expanded many raw data incbins, especially in asm/sdata.s and asm/data.s, into explicit .4byte/.asciz-style assembly data and replaced several embedded address constants with symbolic label or function references. The PR primarily improved data readability and relocation/label resolution in the assembly corpus rather than decompiling C code. There was no recorded PR body or review discussion, so intent beyond the title and diff is inferred from the changes.
what_changed: Removed many .incbin directives from asm/sdata.s and replaced them with explicit assembled data. The file had 814 additions and 312 deletions across 75 hunks.
Expanded several data ranges in asm/data.s from raw incbins into .4byte constants, strings encoded as words, NULLs, and symbolic pointer tables. The file had 1877 additions and 21 deletions.
Added global labels around formerly anonymous data regions, such as lbl_803BAFE8, lbl_803FA3F4, lbl_803FA404, lbl_803FA414, lbl_803FA424, lbl_803FA434, lbl_803FB064, and lbl_803FB074.

Resolved some data pointers to named labels or functions instead of leaving them as raw incbin bytes, for example .4byte lbl_804D5860, .4byte lbl_804D5868, .4byte lbl_804D5870, .4byte func_80392194, .4byte func_80395644, .4byte func_803956D8, .4byte func_803957C0, and .4byte func_80395A78.
Resolved at least one jump table from raw data into symbolic branch-target labels, such as jtbl_8040B870 entries pointing at lbl_80394B94 through lbl_80394DC4.
Added labels in assembly code files, including asm/sysdolphin/baselib/code_80391580.s and asm/melee/text_2.s, likely so data tables could point to exact code labels instead of numeric addresses.
smart_moves: Converted opaque incbin regions into assembler-visible constants without attempting to over-name or over-type data that was not yet understood.
Used existing address-style labels such as lbl_803FA128 and jtbl_8040B870 consistently, preserving match-oriented naming while still making data searchable.
Resolved known pointer entries to symbols where possible, which is more maintainable than raw address words and helps future cross-reference work.
Introduced labels at internal code targets before using them in data tables, avoiding unresolved symbolic references and making jump/function pointer tables clearer.
Kept many values as .4byte when semantic structure was uncertain, which reduces the risk of inventing incorrect C-like types during early data cleanup.
decomp_lessons: When removing incbins from data sections, preserve exact word order and alignment first; semantic naming can come later once references and structures are better understood.
Pointer-heavy data tables should be rewritten with symbolic .4byte label references whenever the target is known. This improves searchability and makes later C extraction easier.
Jump tables are especially valuable to deincbin because replacing raw bytes with .4byte lbl_* entries exposes code-control structure for future function work.
Large raw data blocks can be incrementally improved by splitting them into labeled islands, strings, numeric constants, and pointer arrays without requiring full reverse engineering of the structure.
Hex-encoded string words may be acceptable as an intermediate step when exact layout matters or when strings are embedded in fixed-width records rather than simple null-terminated .asciz blobs.
naming_conventions: Address-derived labels use lbl_XXXXXXXX with uppercase hex in filenames/function symbols where already present, for example lbl_803FA128 and lbl_8040B870.
Jump table labels use the jtbl_XXXXXXXX prefix, as shown by jtbl_8040B870 and jtbl_8040B914.
Function references keep the func_XXXXXXXX convention, such as func_80392194 and func_80395644.
No evidence of new semantic names being introduced; this PR mainly used existing decomp placeholder conventions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace .incbin ranges with exact .4byte sequences to keep binary layout stable while exposing contents to the assembler.
Use .global before newly exposed data labels so other assembly files or pointer tables can reference them.
Represent NULL pointers explicitly as .4byte NULL rather than raw zero when pointer context is known.
Resolve raw pointer words to symbolic labels/functions when the destination can be identified.
For branch tables, emit .4byte lbl_* entries in table order instead of preserving raw table bytes.
Add labels to code files at referenced branch/function targets so data tables can use symbols instead of numeric addresses.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, reviews, or PR body text were present in the provided context.
There is no direct reviewer guidance to extract; conclusions are based on the title, changed-file list, and diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace the newly exposed lbl_803FA* and lbl_8040B* data with typed C structures or semantic names.
Search for references to jtbl_8040B870 and jtbl_8040B914 to identify the switch functions whose control flow was clarified by this PR.
Audit remaining .incbin directives in asm/data.s and asm/sdata.s after PR 88 to identify the next easiest deincbin targets.
Check whether raw address words such as 0x803FAB04-style entries in expanded data were later converted to lbl_* references.
